source: verify.py failure this session — test_integration crashed on Windows cp1252.
title: File reads without encoding='utf-8' crash on Windows cp1252 (← char in pi_agent.py)
what_failed: testing/test_integration.py::test_integration_pi_agent_imports did open(path,'r').read() with no encoding; Windows defaults to cp1252 which can't decode the '←' (U+2190) char in pi_agent.py's chats-command display, raising UnicodeDecodeError byte 0x90.
why_likely: Implicit platform-default encoding on open(); the non-ASCII arrow entered via the T-186 'chats' command display.
resolution: Added encoding='utf-8' to the test's file read. RESIDUAL: a repo-wide sweep for other open() calls lacking encoding= is recommended (tracked here as a note; low incidence since most code uses Read/Write helpers).
analysis: Classic Windows landmine. Always pass encoding='utf-8' on text open(). Verified: test passes.
